svg.text: TAGALOG
HISTORY & THE PHILIPPINES
AUSTRONESIAN ARRIVAL
~2200 BCE: Austronesian speakers arrive in Philippines from Taiwan
Proto-Philippine: ancestor of Tagalog, Cebuano, Ilocano, and 170+ others
Tagalog = "taga-ilog" (people of the river) — Luzon river peoples
Kawi-influenced Baybayin: shows Indian Ocean trade connections
SPANISH COLONIAL PERIOD (1565-1898)
1565: Miguel Lopez de Legazpi founds Spanish settlement in Cebu
Spanish: 4,000+ loanwords in Filipino (kumusta, mesa, pera...)
Latin alphabet replaces Baybayin under Spanish influence
1896: Philippine Revolution — Jose Rizal writes novels in Spanish
1898: Spanish-American War; Philippines becomes US territory
TAGALOG AS NATIONAL LANGUAGE
1937: Institute of National Language chooses Tagalog as basis
1987: Filipino (Tagalog-based) declared official alongside English
~80M speakers; 3rd most widely spoken Asian language
CEBUANO HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
CEBU: FIRST SPANISH CONTACT
1521: Magellan lands in Cebu — first European contact in Philippines
Lapu-Lapu: Cebuano chieftain who killed Magellan in Battle of Mactan
Lapu-Lapu: Philippine national hero; face on 1-centavo coin
1565: Legazpi returns; Cebu becomes first Spanish settlement
CEBUANO SPREAD
Cebuano expanded across Visayas and Mindanao via migration
~20M native speakers; actually MORE than Tagalog L1 speakers
Bisaya identity: strong regional pride, distinct from Manila Tagalog
TAGALOG vs. CEBUANO RIVALRY
Cebuano speakers resented Tagalog being chosen as national base
Cebuano: more speakers L1, but geographically dispersed
Tagalog: centered in Manila (political capital)
Today: Filipino (Tagalog-based) taught in all Philippine schools
Cebuano: remains vibrant; Sto. Nino shrine keeps Cebu as spiritual center
Both peoples: Austronesian kin, 500 years of shared colonial history
PLATE 42d ALT · TAGALOG / CEBUANO · HISTORY
